message:
Add timeline component and finalize session work

This commit adds final polish for the current development session:

TUI Timeline Component (internal/tui/components/timeline.go):
- Visual timeline for request lifecycle events
- Shows: creation, reviews, execution, completion
- Timestamp display with relative time
- Status transitions with icons

CLI Review Updates (internal/cli/review.go):
- Minor refinements to review command formatting
- Enhanced output for different-model requirements

Beads Updates (.beads/beads.base.jsonl):
- Track progress on concurrent agent work
- Updated status for various implementation beads

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement the beautiful lipgloss-styled quick reference card shown when running slb with no arguments.

## Layout (from plan)
Box drawing with rounded corners, Catppuccin Mocha colors:
- Title: gradient mauve to blue
- Commands: green (#a6e3a1)
- Flags: yellow (#f9e2af)
- Tier badges: 🔴 red, 🟠 orange, 🟡 yellow

## Features
- Responsive: adjusts to terminal width (min 72, max 100)
- ASCII fallback for terminals without Unicode
- Shows SETUP, REQUESTOR, REVIEWER sections
- Shows tier legend and key flags

## Files
- internal/cli/root.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Card renders with lipgloss styling
- [ ] Colors match Catppuccin Mocha
- [ ] Responsive to terminal width
- [ ] ASCII fallback works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:47:32.923951078-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T19:34:33.782152291-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T19:34:33.782152291-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-0un","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-v0z.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:49:29.214422221-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-1c3","content_hash":"c3cd63bba07b536885265aead241dc7f4196a4aab87f75bd4838fcf67df4f488","title":"EPIC: Configuration System","description":"## Overview
Implement the hierarchical configuration system using TOML.

## Background & Rationale
Configuration flows from multiple sources with clear precedence:
1. Built-in defaults (hardcoded sensible defaults)
2. User config (~/.slb/config.toml)
3. Project config (.slb/config.toml)
4. Environment variables (SLB_*)
5. Command-line flags (highest priority)

## Configuration Sections (from plan)

### [general]
- min_approvals, require_different_model, different_model_timeout
- conflict_resolution (any_rejection_blocks | first_wins | human_breaks_tie)
- request_timeout, approval_ttl_minutes, approval_ttl_critical_minutes
- timeout_action, enable_dry_run, enable_rollback_capture
- cross_project_reviews, review_pool

### [daemon]
- use_file_watcher, ipc_socket, tcp_addr, tcp_require_auth, tcp_allowed_ips
- log_level, pid_file

### [rate_limits]
- max_pending_per_session, max_requests_per_minute
- rate_limit_action (reject | queue | warn)

### [notifications]
- desktop_enabled, desktop_delay_seconds
- webhook_url, email_enabled

### [history]
- database_path, git_repo_path, retention_days, auto_git_commit

### [patterns.critical/dangerous/caution/safe]
- min_approvals, patterns array, dynamic_quorum settings

### [integrations]
- agent_mail_enabled, agent_mail_thread
- claude_hooks_enabled

### [agents]
- trusted_self_approve, trusted_self_approve_delay_seconds
- blocked agents list

## Files
- internal/config/config.go (struct definitions)
- internal/config/defaults.go
- internal/config/loader.go (TOML loading, hierarchy)
- internal/config/patterns.go (pattern-specific config)
- internal/cli/config.go

## Success Criteria
1. Config hierarchy works correctly
2. Environment variables override file config
3. CLI flags override everything
4. Config validation catches invalid values
5. `slb config` commands work","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:14:18.102283788-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:14:18.102283788-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-1c3","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-v0z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:14:18.10392258-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-1c3.1","content_hash":"d40069003704426bc0a895b20973500c171b54e2b21e6de03ffa5250be54adc3","title":"Implement configuration loading and CLI commands","description":"## Task
Implement the hierarchical configuration system with ALL sections from the plan.

## Configuration Hierarchy (lowest to highest priority)
1. Built-in defaults (hardcoded)
2. User config (~/.slb/config.toml)
3. Project config (.slb/config.toml)
4. Environment variables (SLB_*)
5. Command-line flags

## Config Struct Sections (ALL from plan)

### [general]
- min_approvals (int, default 2)
- require_different_model (bool, default false)
- different_model_timeout (int, 300)
- conflict_resolution ("any_rejection_blocks"|"first_wins"|"human_breaks_tie")
- request_timeout (int, 1800)
- approval_ttl_minutes (int, 30)
- approval_ttl_critical_minutes (int, 10)
- timeout_action ("escalate"|"auto_reject"|"auto_approve_warn")
- enable_dry_run (bool, true)
- enable_rollback_capture (bool, true)
- max_rollback_size_mb (int, 100)
- cross_project_reviews (bool, false)
- review_pool ([]string)

### [daemon]
- use_file_watcher (bool, true)
- ipc_socket (string)
- tcp_addr (string, empty)
- tcp_require_auth (bool, true)
- tcp_allowed_ips ([]string)
- log_level (string, "info")
- pid_file (string)

### [rate_limits]
- max_pending_per_session (int, 5)
- max_requests_per_minute (int, 10)
- rate_limit_action ("reject"|"queue"|"warn")

### [notifications]
- desktop_enabled (bool, true)
- desktop_delay_seconds (int, 60)
- webhook_url (string)
- email_enabled (bool, false)

### [history]
- database_path (string)
- git_repo_path (string)
- retention_days (int, 365)
- auto_git_commit (bool, true)

### [patterns.critical/dangerous/caution/safe]
- min_approvals (int)
- dynamic_quorum (bool)
- dynamic_quorum_floor (int)
- auto_approve_delay_seconds (int)
- patterns ([]string)

### [integrations]
- agent_mail_enabled (bool, true)
- agent_mail_thread (string, "SLB-Reviews")
- claude_hooks_enabled (bool, true)

### [agents]
- trusted_self_approve ([]string)
- trusted_self_approve_delay_seconds (int, 300)
- blocked ([]string)

## CLI Commands
- slb config → show current effective config
- slb config get <key> → get specific value
- slb config set <key> <value> → set in project config
- slb config edit → open in $EDITOR
- slb config --global → operate on user config

## Files
- internal/config/config.go
- internal/config/defaults.go
- internal/config/loader.go
- internal/config/validation.go
- internal/cli/config.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All config sections implemented
- [ ] Hierarchy works correctly
- [ ] Env var override works
- [ ] CLI flags override everything
- [ ] Validation catches invalid values","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:27:52.756209905-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:27:02.244002094-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:27:02.244007814-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-1c3.1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-v0z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:27:52.757367451-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9","content_hash":"07b35ca114d28cd772000e17a12e42fc2280b6d4e6bed7557ffc00158ef90fd0","title":"EPIC: TUI Dashboard (Human Interface)","description":"## Overview
Implement the beautiful TUI dashboard using Charmbracelet ecosystem - the ONLY interactive human interface.

## Background & Rationale
Humans need a visual interface to:
- Monitor multiple agents and pending requests
- Review and approve/reject commands
- Search history
- Manage configuration
- Handle escalations

The TUI is NOT for agents. Agents use the CLI with --json.

## Key Views (from plan)

### Dashboard View (Main)
- Left panel: Active agents with status indicators
- Center panel: Pending requests with risk tier badges
- Right panel: Recent activity
- Bottom bar: Stats (24h approved, rejected, avg response time)

### Request Detail View
- Command display with syntax highlighting
- Risk tier and status badges
- Requestor info (agent, program, model)
- Full justification (reason, effect, goal, safety)
- Dry-run output (if available)
- Attachments list
- Reviews list
- Action buttons: Approve, Reject, Copy command

### History Browser View
- Filters: status, agent, date range
- FTS search across command, reason, etc.
- Paginated results table
- Click-through to detail view

### Pattern Removal Review
- Pending pattern removal requests
- Human can approve/reject

## Visual Design (from plan)
- Catppuccin color themes (mocha, macchiato, latte, nord)
- Nerd Font icons with Unicode/ASCII fallbacks
- Consistent color coding: 🔴 CRITICAL, 🟠 DANGEROUS, 🟡 CAUTION, 🟢 SAFE
- Responsive layouts (80 col minimum)
- Keyboard-first with mouse support
- Real-time updates via daemon

## Files
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go, panels/, keybindings.go
- internal/tui/request/detail.go, approve.go, reject.go
- internal/tui/history/browser.go, filters.go
- internal/tui/components/commandbox.go, statusbadge.go, riskindicator.go, etc.
- internal/tui/icons/icons.go
- internal/tui/styles/styles.go, gradients.go, shimmer.go
- internal/tui/theme/theme.go

## Success Criteria
1. Dashboard shows real-time agent and request status
2. Request detail view displays all information clearly
3. Approve/reject works from TUI
4. History search works with FTS
5. Keyboard navigation covers all actions
6. Responsive to terminal width
7. Themes apply consistently","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:13:54.922031239-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:13:54.922031239-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:13:54.923443259-06:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f4c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:13:54.924328538-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9.1","content_hash":"b63c881d5f198c1ad8f08b30801d00df780f4df161eea55fc0fbf0b53a50de2c","title":"Implement TUI dashboard main view","description":"## Task

- [ ] Real-time updates work
- [ ] Responsive to terminal width","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:23:31.545182217-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:23:31.545182217-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9.1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f5d.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:23:31.546243043-06:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9.1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:31:41.136373073-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9.2","content_hash":"da1adef96dcf269898411da849219e75a7ccf34fc4b9e6eef29b544f3c420f59","title":"Implement TUI request detail view","description":"## Task
Implement the request detail view for reviewing and approving/rejecting.

## Layout (from plan)
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Request Detail                               🟠 DANGEROUS • PENDING │

│ Reason: Removing stale build artifacts after failed compilation     │
│ Expected Effect: ./build directory deleted (~500MB)                 │

│ Would remove:                                                       │
│   ./build/cache/ (245 files)                                        │
│   ./build/dist/ (12 files)                                          │
│                                                                     │
│ ── Reviews (0/1 required) ─────────────────────────────────────────  │
│ No reviews yet.                                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [a]pprove  [r]eject  [c]opy command  [ESC] back                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

## Features
- Syntax highlighting for command
- Scrollable sections

- internal/tui/request/detail.go
- internal/tui/request/approve.go (approval form)
- internal/tui/request/reject.go (rejection form)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Dry-run output shown
- [ ] Reviews listed
- [ ] Approve/reject actions work
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:23:49.217532275-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:23:49.217532275-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9.2","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-2t9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:23:49.218536526-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.3","content_hash":"f10919798f67905941025384d9cce887fbda3ddb4ac150b07fe04575627a670b","title":"Implement graceful degradation when daemon unavailable","description":"## Task
Ensure slb CLI works even when daemon is not running, with clear messaging.

## Background (from plan)
Commands should check daemon status before requiring it. When daemon is unavailable:
- Request still works, just without real-time features
- Clear warning shown to user
- File-based polling fallback

## What Works Without Daemon
- slb init, version, config
- slb session start/end/list
- slb request (creates request file, no notifications)
- slb pending, review, approve, reject
- slb execute
- slb patterns *
- slb history, show

## What Degrades Without Daemon
- slb watch → falls back to file polling
- slb tui → works but no real-time updates
- Desktop notifications disabled
- Agent Mail notifications disabled
- Fast IPC unavailable (file polling instead)

## Functions (internal/daemon/client.go)

### IsDaemonRunning() bool
- Check PID file exists
- Verify process is alive (kill -0)
- Try to connect to IPC socket

### WithDaemonOrFallback(fn func(), fallback func())
- If daemon running: call fn
- Else: show warning, call fallback

### Common Warning Message
"Warning: slb daemon not running. Some features disabled.
Start daemon with: slb daemon start
Continuing with file-based polling..."

## Implementation Points
1. Check daemon status at start of relevant commands
2. If unavailable, log warning to stderr
3. Continue with degraded functionality
4. Never fail hard just because daemon is down

## Files
- internal/daemon/client.go
- Modifications to relevant CLI commands

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All core commands work without daemon
- [ ] Clear warning messages shown
- [ ] File-based fallback works
- [ ] No hard failures due to missing daemon
- [ ] slb watch polls files when no daemon","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:02.223231857-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T19:57:02.573114807-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T19:57:02.573114807-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.3","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f4c.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:02.224207314-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.4","content_hash":"d20daa702f41eb7daefda1b422ba14ef68a0b1d3848dffa66bef50967c5b7644","title":"Implement history, show, and version CLI commands","description":"## Task
Implement the history browsing and detail viewing CLI commands.

## Commands

### slb history
Browse and search request history.

Flags:
- --query/-q: FTS search query
- --status: Filter by status (pending, approved, rejected, executed, etc.)
- --agent: Filter by agent name
- --tier: Filter by risk tier
- --since: Only show requests after date
- --limit: Max results (default 50)
- --format: Output format (table, json)

JSON output: Array of request summaries

### slb show <request-id>
Show detailed information about a specific request.

Flags:
- --with-reviews: Include full review details
- --with-execution: Include execution details
- --with-attachments: Include attachment content

JSON output: Full request object with nested data

### slb version
Show version and build information.

Flags:
- --json: Output as JSON
- --check: Check for updates (optional, future)

Output:
slb version X.Y.Z
  Built: 2025-12-13
  Commit: abc123
  Go: go1.25
  Config: ~/.slb/config.toml
  Project: .slb/state.db

JSON:
{
  "version": "X.Y.Z",
  "commit": "abc123",
  "build_date": "2025-12-13",
  "go_version": "go1.25",
  "config_paths": {...}
}

## Files
- internal/cli/history.go
- internal/cli/show.go
- internal/cli/version.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] history search with FTS works
- [ ] show displays full details
- [ ] version shows build info
- [ ] JSON output for all commands
- [ ] Filters work correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:22.678792898-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:20:07.539157277-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:20:07.539157277-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.4","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-3s8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:22.679741856-06:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.4","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:22.680615653-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-9ea","content_hash":"39f3c67c4175784b33a08fc00125a9a8527f3c0c04052e92723ffbf744d5a918","title":"Implement webhook notifications for CRITICAL requests","description":"## Task
Implement webhook notifications for Slack/Discord/etc integration.

## Background (from plan [notifications] section)
webhook_url config option allows posting to external services.

## Webhook Payload
POST to configured URL with JSON:
{
  "event": "critical_request_pending",
  "request_id": "req-abc",
  "command": "<truncated>",
  "tier": "critical",
  "requestor": "AgentName",
  "url": "https://..."
}

## Configuration
[notifications]
webhook_url = "https://hooks.slack.com/..."

## Files
- internal/daemon/notifications.go (extend)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST sent to webhook_url
- [ ] Timeout handling
- [ ] Error logging (don't fail on webhook error)
- [ ] Configurable per tier","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:48:03.11215245-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:48:03.11215245-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-9ea","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f4c.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:48:03.114309618-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-anv","content_hash":"c8eaca3e023ee80112419f0a312e8d16a2d322eab66478b430aec22936bf2385","title":"Implement cross-project review support","description":"## Task
Implement support for reviewers to see/approve requests from other projects.

## Background (from plan [general] section)
cross_project_reviews = false  # When true, allows cross-project visibility
review_pool = []               # Projects that share a review pool

## Features
1. slb pending --all-projects: Show requests from all projects
2. slb pending --review-pool: Show from configured pool only
3. Review/approve/reject works cross-project
4. User-level daemon monitors all .slb/ directories

## Configuration
[general]
cross_project_reviews = true
review_pool = ["/data/myapp", "/data/myapp-infra"]

## Use Case
Agent working on myapp-infra can approve dangerous commands from myapp.

## Files
- internal/db/requests.go (cross-project queries)
- internal/cli/pending.go (--all-projects, --review-pool flags)
- internal/daemon/daemon.go (multi-project monitoring)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --all-projects shows cross-project requests
- [ ] --review-pool respects configuration
- [ ] Approve/reject works cross-project

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19","content_hash":"d19d980e88c6c9a67d0889a8e0c831748b6dbb4055dcea3272b3722504e4277c","title":"EPIC: External Integrations","description":"## Overview
Implement integrations with external tools and platforms: Claude Code hooks, Agent Mail, Cursor rules, etc.

## Background & Rationale
slb must integrate into the existing AI coding agent ecosystem. Key integrations:
1. Claude Code hooks - intercept dangerous commands before execution
2. MCP Agent Mail - unified coordination channel
3. Cursor rules - instructions for Cursor agents

## Claude Code Hooks (from plan)

Generate `.claude/hooks.json`:
{
  "hooks": {
    "pre_bash": {
      "command": "slb patterns test --exit-code",
      "input": { "command": "${COMMAND}" },
      "on_block": { "message": "This command requires slb approval..." }
    }
  }
}

The `--exit-code` flag makes `slb patterns test` return non-zero if approval needed.

Command: `slb integrations claude-hooks --install`

## Agent Mail Integration

When request is created:
- Send notification to "SLB-Reviews" thread
- Importance based on risk tier
- Include command, justification, links to approve/reject

SLB-System virtual sender with broadcast to SLB-Broadcast.

## Cursor Rules

Generate `.cursorrules` section with:
- Pattern checking instructions
- Request workflow steps
- Link to slb documentation

Command: `slb integrations cursor-rules --install`

## Files
- internal/integrations/agentmail.go
- internal/integrations/claudehooks.go
- internal/integrations/cursor.go
- internal/cli/integrations.go

## Success Criteria
1. Claude Code hooks generated correctly
2. Agent Mail messages sent on request creation
3. Cursor rules generate proper markdown
4. All integrations are optional and configurable","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:14:34.665328919-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:14:34.665328919-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:14:34.666659698-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19.1","content_hash":"310326ffe173bf80261cbff86daf2d0b4003539f34f7c9f89c9240306bc27535","title":"Implement Claude Code hooks generator","description":"## Task
Implement generator for Claude Code pre_bash hooks.

## Command: slb integrations claude-hooks --install

## Generated Hook (.claude/hooks.json)
{
  "hooks": {
    "pre_bash": {
      "command": "slb patterns test --exit-code",
      "input": {
        "command": "${COMMAND}"
      },
      "on_block": {
        "message": "This command requires slb approval. Use: slb request \"${COMMAND}\" --reason \"...\" --expected-effect \"...\" --goal \"...\" --safety \"...\""
      }
    }
  }
}

## How it Works
1. `slb patterns test --exit-code` returns:
   - 0 if command doesn't need approval
   - 1 if command needs approval
2. Claude Code intercepts exit code 1
3. Shows on_block message to agent
4. Agent must use slb workflow

## Options
- --install: Write to .claude/hooks.json
- --preview: Show what would be written
- --merge: Merge with existing hooks.json

## Files
- internal/integrations/claudehooks.go
- internal/cli/integrations.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generates valid hooks.json
- [ ] Merges with existing hooks
- [ ] Creates .claude/ directory
- [ ] Preview mode works","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:26:17.694676008-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:09:52.591580546-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:09:52.591580546-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19.1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:26:17.695639883-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19.2","content_hash":"a78aea21c4c2f00f7ae5dbe7189ed7e8784a9f6bb8274eae2202f82ca4b1fd43","title":"Implement Agent Mail integration","description":"## Task
Implement Agent Mail integration for cross-agent notifications.

## When Request Created
Send notification to SLB-Reviews thread with:
- Command (truncated/redacted)
- Risk tier
- Requestor info
- Justification summary
- Links to approve/reject

## Message Format (from plan)
{
  "project_key": "<project_path>",
  "sender_name": "SLB-System",
  "to": ["SLB-Broadcast"],
  "subject": "[SLB] DANGEROUS: rm -rf ./build...",
  "body_md": "## Command Approval Request\n\n**ID**: req-abc\n**Risk**: dangerous\n**Command**: `rm -rf ./build`\n\n### Justification\n...\n\n---\nTo review: `slb review req-abc`\nTo approve: `slb approve req-abc --session-id <your-session>`",
  "importance": "normal",
  "thread_id": "SLB-Reviews"
}

## Importance Mapping
- CRITICAL → "urgent"
- DANGEROUS → "normal"
- CAUTION → "low"

### NotifyRequestApproved(req *Request, review *Review) error
### NotifyRequestRejected(req *Request, review *Review) error
### NotifyRequestExecuted(req *Request, result *ExecutionResult) error

## Configuration
[integrations]
agent_mail_enabled = true
agent_mail_thread = "SLB-Reviews"

## Files
- internal/integrations/agentmail.go

- [ ] Notifications sent on request creation
- [ ] Thread grouping works
- [ ] Importance set correctly
- [ ] Redacted commands in messages
- [ ] Configurable enable/disable","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:26:33.636090057-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:26:33.636090057-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-d19.2","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-3s8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:26:33.636990294-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.1","content_hash":"376daf481863cfe2b80b0917ab76e6d7793e71adb49f38ff1c79efb3bcadce15","title":"Implement command execution core logic","description":"## Task
Implement the core logic for executing approved commands.

## Key Design Decision (v2.0.0)
Client-side execution! The command runs in the CALLER'S shell environment, not in the daemon. This is critical for inheriting:
- AWS_PROFILE, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
- KUBECONFIG
- VIRTUAL_ENV, PATH modifications
- SSH_AUTH_SOCK
- Database connection strings

## Functions (internal/core/execute.go)

### ExecuteApprovedRequest(opts ExecuteOptions) (*ExecutionResult, error)
Options:
- RequestID (required)
- SessionID (required for tracking)
- Timeout (duration, default 5 min)
- Background (bool)

Execution Gate Conditions (all must pass):
1. Request status is APPROVED
2. approval_expires_at has not elapsed
3. command_hash still matches (no mutation)
4. Current pattern policy doesn't raise required tier
5. First executor wins (idempotent)

Logic:
1. Validate all gate conditions
2. Update status to EXECUTING
3. Execute command in current shell environment
4. Capture stdout/stderr to log file
5. On completion, update status to EXECUTED/EXECUTION_FAILED
6. Record exit code, duration
7. Return result

### RunCommand(spec *CommandSpec, timeout time.Duration) (*CommandResult, error)
- Execute using exec.Command or shell
- Stream output to terminal AND log file
- Handle timeout with context
- Return exit code, captured output

## Files
- internal/core/execute.go
- internal/core/command.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gate conditions all checked
- [ ] Command inherits caller's env
- [ ] Output logged to file
- [ ] Timeout kills command
- [ ] Status updated on completion","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueBear","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:19:55.870469078-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:12:37.713908484-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:12:37.713912592-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-3s8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:19:55.871476545-06:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-jrn.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:19:55.872330846-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.2","content_hash":"21b77e892762784d7c2a1a6e163093321ec63fcbb5571280c46f4b2a30b69405","title":"Implement execute CLI commands","description":"## Task
Implement CLI commands for executing approved requests.

## Commands

### slb execute <request-id>
- Flags: --session-id/-s
- Flags: --timeout (seconds, default 300)
- Flags: --background (don't wait, return PID)
- Validates gate conditions
- Executes in caller's environment
- JSON: { exit_code, duration_ms, log_path }

### slb emergency-execute "<command>"
- Human override that bypasses approval
- Flags: --reason (required)
- Flags: --capture-rollback
- Flags: --yes (skip interactive confirmation)
- Flags: --ack "<sha256(command)>" (required with --yes)
- Requires interactive confirmation OR --yes --ack
- Logs extensively for audit
- JSON: { exit_code, logged_at, reason }

### slb rollback <request-id>
- Requires rollback data was captured
- Restores captured state
- JSON: { request_id, rolled_back_at }

## Error Cases
- Not approved: "Request is not in approved state"
- Approval expired: "Approval has expired, please re-request"
- Hash mismatch: "Command was modified since approval"
- Already executed: "Request has already been executed"

## Files
- internal/cli/execute.go
- internal/cli/emergency.go
- internal/cli/rollback.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Execute validates all gate conditions
- [ ] Command runs in caller's environment
- [ ] Emergency requires confirmation or --yes --ack
- [ ] Rollback restores captured state
- [ ] All output is JSON compatible","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueBear","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:20:11.282654838-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:15:41.491540745-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:15:41.491545985-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.2","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:20:11.283601211-06:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.2","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-v0z.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:20:11.284485618-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.3","content_hash":"d9c8f44b45e99139fd5a40bc0536a0c8e0f01275f1a1dcb755a002f684126f25","title":"Implement dry-run pre-flight for supported commands","description":"## Task
Implement dry-run pre-flight checks for commands that support it.

## Supported Commands (from plan)

### kubectl
- Original: kubectl delete deployment foo
- Dry-run: kubectl delete deployment foo --dry-run=client
- Output: Shows what would be deleted

### terraform
- Original: terraform destroy
- Dry-run: terraform plan -destroy
- Output: Shows resources that would be destroyed

### rm
- Original: rm -rf ./build
- Dry-run: ls -la ./build (show what would be removed)
- Output: File listing with sizes

### git reset
- Original: git reset --hard HEAD~5
- Dry-run: git diff HEAD~5..HEAD
- Output: Shows commits that would be lost

### helm
- Original: helm uninstall myrelease
- Dry-run: helm get manifest myrelease
- Output: Shows resources that would be removed

## Functions (internal/core/dryrun.go)

### RunDryRun(spec *CommandSpec) (*DryRunResult, error)
1. Detect command type from spec
2. Generate dry-run variant
3. Execute dry-run command
4. Capture and format output
5. Return result

### GetDryRunCommand(cmd string) (string, bool)
- Return dry-run variant if supported
- Return false if no dry-run available

## Files
- internal/core/dryrun.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] kubectl --dry-run works
- [ ] terraform plan -destroy works
- [ ] rm shows file listing
- [ ] git shows diff
- [ ] Graceful fallback for unsupported","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownLake","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:25:05.04879799-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:29:30.424921253-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:27:37.927951738-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.3","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-kb5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:25:05.049776051-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8","content_hash":"ef509b6c7980b33adc3005dce58e1fce3e9af7d7ef0dc387f90fa5884869974a","title":"EPIC: Pattern Matching & Risk Classification","description":"## Overview
Implement the three-tier risk classification system that determines which commands need approval and at what level.

## Background & Rationale
Not all dangerous commands are equally dangerous. The plan defines three tiers:
- CRITICAL: Catastrophic, irreversible (2+ approvals, human notification)
- DANGEROUS: Significant, recoverable with effort (1 approval)
- CAUTION: Annoying if wrong, easily fixed (auto-approved after 30s)

Plus a SAFE tier for commands that skip review entirely.

## Key Features (from plan)
1. **Pattern Matching**: Regex patterns per tier, case-insensitive
2. **Command Normalization**: Strip sudo/env wrappers, detect compound commands
3. **Path Resolution**: Expand relative paths to absolute for safety checks
4. **Pattern Precedence**: SAFE → CRITICAL → DANGEROUS → CAUTION
5. **Agent Pattern Management**: Agents can ADD patterns (making things safer) but CANNOT remove
6. **Dynamic Quorum**: If fewer agents available, adjust required approvals

## Key Commands
- `slb patterns list [--tier <tier>]` → list patterns
- `slb patterns test "<command>"` / `slb check "<command>"` → show tier
- `slb patterns add --tier <tier> "<pattern>" --reason "..."` → agents can add
- `slb patterns request-removal "<pattern>"` → creates pending removal request
- `slb patterns suggest --tier <tier> "<pattern>"` → propose for human review

## Files
- internal/core/patterns.go (matching logic)
- internal/config/patterns.go (default patterns)
- internal/cli/patterns.go
- internal/db/pattern_changes.go, custom_patterns.go

## Default Pattern Categories
- File System: rm -rf, chmod -R, chown -R
- Git: reset --hard, clean -fd, push --force
- Kubernetes: delete node/namespace/pv/pvc
- Databases: DROP DATABASE/SCHEMA/TABLE, DELETE without WHERE
- Cloud: terraform destroy, aws terminate-instances
- Containers: docker system prune -a

## Success Criteria
1. Commands correctly classified into tiers
2. Normalization handles sudo, env, compound commands
3. Agents can add but not remove patterns
4. Pattern test returns JSON with needs_approval, tier, min_approvals","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:11:57.291380377-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:11:57.291380377-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:11:57.29266506-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8.1","content_hash":"a5ed0fb46ca799f0dd37ffeeed7b1345b2fee6c052cf601897dceb2c19c36daf","title":"Implement pattern matching engine with command normalization","description":"## Task
Implement the pattern matching engine that classifies commands into risk tiers.

## Core Functions (internal/core/patterns.go)

### ClassifyCommand(cmd string, cwd string) (RiskTier, *MatchResult)
- Normalize command
- Check tiers in order: SAFE → CRITICAL → DANGEROUS → CAUTION
- Return tier and match details

### NormalizeCommand(cmd string) (*NormalizedCommand, error)
- Parse with shell-aware tokenizer
- Extract primary command from wrappers: sudo, doas, env, command, builtin, time, nice, ionice, nohup
- Detect compound commands (;, &&, ||, |, subshells)
- If any segment matches tier, whole command is at least that tier
- If parsing fails, fall back to raw regex and upgrade tier by one step

### MatchPattern(pattern, cmd string) bool
- Regex matching, case-insensitive by default
- Handle special characters

### ResolvePathsInCommand(cmd, cwd string) string
- Expand relative paths (./,../) to absolute
- Used for path-aware patterns

## Pattern Precedence
1. Check SAFE patterns → skip review entirely
2. Check CRITICAL → highest risk
3. Check DANGEROUS
4. Check CAUTION
5. No match → allowed without review

## Match Result
- Tier
- MatchedPattern
- MinApprovals
- NeedsApproval (bool)

## Files
- internal/core/patterns.go
- internal/core/normalize.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Commands correctly classified
- [ ] sudo/env wrappers stripped
- [ ] Compound commands detected
- [ ] Paths resolved correctly
- [ ] Case-insensitive matching","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:17:09.132220861-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T19:39:45.690271534-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T19:39:45.690271534-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8.1","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-f5d.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:31:34.904807286-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-qr8.2","content_hash":"2c9dfc5d8d592835573ae38884c0116112f484e2698979430d451e53b352bb2e","title":"Define default dangerous patterns","description":"## Task
Define the default pattern sets for each risk tier as specified in the plan.

## Pattern Categories (internal/config/patterns.go)

### CRITICAL (2+ approvals required)
File System:
- ^rm\s+-rf\s+/(?!tmp) - rm -rf / (but not /tmp)
- ^rm\s+-rf\s+~ - rm -rf ~

Databases:
- DROP\s+DATABASE
- DROP\s+SCHEMA
- TRUNCATE\s+TABLE
- DELETE\s+FROM\s+[\w.`"[\]]+\s*($|;|--|/\*) - DELETE without WHERE

Kubernetes:
- ^kubectl\s+delete\s+(node|namespace|pv|pvc)
- ^helm\s+uninstall.*--all

Cloud:
- ^terraform\s+destroy(?!.*-target) - terraform destroy without -target
- ^aws\s+.*terminate-instances
- ^gcloud.*delete.*--quiet

Git:
- ^git\s+push.*--force(?!-with-lease) - force push (not with-lease)

Containers:
- ^docker\s+system\s+prune\s+-a

### DANGEROUS (1 approval required)
- ^rm\s+-rf, ^rm\s+-r
- ^git\s+reset\s+--hard, ^git\s+clean\s+-fd
- ^git\s+push.*--force-with-lease
- ^kubectl\s+delete
- ^helm\s+uninstall
- ^docker\s+rm, ^docker\s+rmi
- ^terraform\s+destroy.*-target
- ^terraform\s+state\s+rm
- DROP\s+TABLE
- DELETE\s+FROM.*WHERE
- ^chmod\s+-R, ^chown\s+-R

### CAUTION (auto-approved after 30s)
- ^rm\s+[^-] - rm without flags
- ^git\s+stash\s+drop
- ^git\s+branch\s+-[dD]
- ^npm\s+uninstall, ^pip\s+uninstall, ^cargo\s+remove

### SAFE (skip review)
- ^rm\s+.*\.log$, ^rm\s+.*\.tmp$, ^rm\s+.*\.bak$
- ^git\s+stash(?!.*drop)
- ^kubectl\s+delete\s+pod
- ^npm\s+cache\s+clean

## Files
- internal/config/patterns.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All default patterns defined
- [ ] Patterns compile as valid regex
- [ ] Tier configuration includes min_approvals

@@ -0,0 +1,279 @@
+// Package components provides timeline components for request lifecycle.
+package components
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/slb/internal/tui/theme"
+)
+
+// TimelineEvent represents a single event in the request timeline.
+type TimelineEvent struct {
+	State     string
+	Timestamp time.Time
+	Actor     string // Who performed this action
+	Details   string // Additional details
+}
+
+// Timeline renders a request lifecycle timeline.
+type Timeline struct {
+	Events    []TimelineEvent
+	Compact   bool
+	Expanded  bool
+	Current   string // Current state to highlight
+}
+
+// NewTimeline creates a new timeline component.
+func NewTimeline() *Timeline {
+	return &Timeline{}
+}
+
+// AddEvent adds an event to the timeline.
+func (t *Timeline) AddEvent(state string, ts time.Time, actor, details string) *Timeline {
+	t.Events = append(t.Events, TimelineEvent{
+		State:     state,
+		Timestamp: ts,
+		Actor:     actor,
+		Details:   details,
+	})
+	return t
+}
+
+// WithCurrent sets the current state to highlight.
+func (t *Timeline) WithCurrent(state string) *Timeline {
+	t.Current = state
+	return t
+}
+
+// AsCompact sets the timeline to compact mode.
+func (t *Timeline) AsCompact() *Timeline {
+	t.Compact = true
+	return t
+}
+
+// AsExpanded sets the timeline to expanded mode.
+func (t *Timeline) AsExpanded() *Timeline {
+	t.Expanded = true
+	return t
+}
+
+// Render renders the timeline.
+func (t *Timeline) Render() string {
+	if t.Compact {
+		return t.renderCompact()
+	}
+	if t.Expanded {
+		return t.renderExpanded()
+	}
+	return t.renderNormal()
+}
+
+// renderCompact renders a single-line compact timeline.
+func (t *Timeline) renderCompact() string {
+	th := theme.Current
+
+	// State order
+	states := []string{"created", "pending", "approved", "executing", "executed"}
+
+	var parts []string
+	activeIdx := -1
+
+	for i, state := range states {
+		// Check if this state has been reached
+		reached := t.hasReachedState(state)
+
+		var color lipgloss.Color
+		if state == t.Current {
+			color = th.Mauve
+			activeIdx = i
+		} else if reached {
+			color = th.Green
+		} else {
+			color = th.Overlay0
+		}
+
+		dot := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(color).Render("●")
+		parts = append(parts, dot)
+	}
+
+	// Connect with arrows
+	result := ""
+	for i, part := range parts {
+		if i > 0 {
+			arrowColor := th.Overlay0
+			if activeIdx >= 0 && i <= activeIdx {
+				arrowColor = th.Green
+			}
+			arrow := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(arrowColor).Render(" → ")
+			result += arrow
+		}
+		result += part
+	}
+
+	return result
+}
+
+// renderNormal renders the standard timeline view.
+func (t *Timeline) renderNormal() string {
+	th := theme.Current
+
+	var lines []string
+
+	for i, event := range t.Events {
+		isLast := i == len(t.Events)-1
+		isCurrent := event.State == t.Current
+
+		// State indicator
+		var stateColor lipgloss.Color
+		switch strings.ToLower(event.State) {
+		case "approved", "executed":
+			stateColor = th.Green
+		case "rejected", "failed":
+			stateColor = th.Red
+		case "pending", "executing":
+			stateColor = th.Blue
+		case "timeout", "escalated":
+			stateColor = th.Yellow
+		default:
+			stateColor = th.Subtext
+		}
+
+		// Build the line
+		connector := "│"
+		node := "●"
+		if isLast {
+			connector = " "
+		}
+		if isCurrent {
+			node = "◉"
+		}
+
+		nodeStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(stateColor).Bold(isCurrent)
+		connectorStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(th.Overlay0)
+
+		stateLabel := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+			Foreground(stateColor).
+			Bold(isCurrent).
+			Render(strings.ToUpper(event.State))
+
+		timeStr := ""
+		if !event.Timestamp.IsZero() {
+			timeStr = lipgloss.NewStyle().
+				Foreground(th.Subtext).
+				Render("  " + event.Timestamp.Format("15:04:05"))
+		}
+
+		line := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s%s",
+			nodeStyle.Render(node),
+			stateLabel,
+			timeStr,
+		)
+		lines = append(lines, line)
+
+		// Add connector to next event
+		if !isLast {
+			lines = append(lines, connectorStyle.Render(connector))
+		}
+	}
+
+	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
+}
+
+// renderExpanded renders the full expanded timeline with details.
+func (t *Timeline) renderExpanded() string {
+	th := theme.Current
+
+	var lines []string
+
+	for i, event := range t.Events {
+		isLast := i == len(t.Events)-1
+		isCurrent := event.State == t.Current
+
+		// State indicator
+		var stateColor lipgloss.Color
+		switch strings.ToLower(event.State) {
+		case "approved", "executed":
+			stateColor = th.Green
+		case "rejected", "failed":
+			stateColor = th.Red
+		case "pending", "executing":
+			stateColor = th.Blue
+		case "timeout", "escalated":
+			stateColor = th.Yellow
+		default:
+			stateColor = th.Subtext
+		}
+
+		nodeStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(stateColor).Bold(isCurrent)
+		connectorStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(th.Overlay0)
+
+		node := "●"
+		if isCurrent {
+			node = "◉"
+		}
+
+		stateLabel := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+			Foreground(stateColor).
+			Bold(isCurrent).
+			Render(strings.ToUpper(event.State))
+
+		// Main line
+		line := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", nodeStyle.Render(node), stateLabel)
+		lines = append(lines, line)
+
+		// Details (indented)
+		if !event.Timestamp.IsZero() {
+			timeStr := event.Timestamp.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")
+			lines = append(lines, connectorStyle.Render("│  ")+
+				lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(th.Subtext).Render(timeStr))
+		}
+
+		if event.Actor != "" {
+			lines = append(lines, connectorStyle.Render("│  ")+
+				lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(th.Subtext).Render("by "+event.Actor))
+		}
+
+		if event.Details != "" {
+			lines = append(lines, connectorStyle.Render("│  ")+
+				lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(th.Text).Render(event.Details))
+		}
+
+		// Connector to next
+		if !isLast {
+			lines = append(lines, connectorStyle.Render("│"))
+		}
+	}
+
+	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
+}
+
+// hasReachedState checks if the timeline has reached a given state.
+func (t *Timeline) hasReachedState(state string) bool {
+	for _, event := range t.Events {
+		if strings.EqualFold(event.State, state) {
+			return true
+		}
+	}
+	return false
+}
+
+// RenderTimeline is a convenience function to create and render a timeline.
+func RenderTimeline(events []TimelineEvent, current string) string {
+	tl := NewTimeline().WithCurrent(current)
+	for _, e := range events {
+		tl.AddEvent(e.State, e.Timestamp, e.Actor, e.Details)
+	}
+	return tl.Render()
+}
+
+// RenderTimelineCompact is a convenience function for compact timeline.
+func RenderTimelineCompact(events []TimelineEvent, current string) string {
+	tl := NewTimeline().WithCurrent(current).AsCompact()
+	for _, e := range events {
+		tl.AddEvent(e.State, e.Timestamp, e.Actor, e.Details)
+	}
+	return tl.Render()
+}
